result.stdout:
Description:
  A CLI tool with commands to help maintain .NET codebases.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  nuget  NuGet maintenance commands
  git    Git repo maintenance workflows
  azdo   Azure DevOps operations

result.stdout:
Description:
  Branch commands

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo branch [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  favorite <branch-name>    Marks a branch as a favorite.
  unfavorite <branch-name>  Removes a branch from favorites.
  pushes <branch-name>      List recent pushes to a branch.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Branch commands

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo branch [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  favorite <branch-name>    Marks a branch as a favorite.
  unfavorite <branch-name>  Removes a branch from favorites.
  pushes <branch-name>      List recent pushes to a branch.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Marks a branch as a favorite.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo branch favorite <branch-name> [options]

Arguments:
  <branch-name>  The name of the branch to mark as a favorite.

Options:
  --what-if                       Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                       Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>   The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)        The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information

  --project <project> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.

result.stdout:
Description:
  List recent pushes to a branch.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo branch pushes <branch-name> [options]

Arguments:
  <branch-name>  The name of the branch to list pushes for.

Options:
  --top <top>                     The maximum number of pushes to return. Defaults to 100.
  --before <before>               Return only pushes that occurred before this timestamp (e.g. 2024-01-15T12:00:00Z).
  --format <Json|Text>            The output format. Defaults to text.
  --what-if                       Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                       Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>   The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)        The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Pull request commands

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  comment <comment>                                                         Post a comment on a pull request.
  create                                                                    Creates a new pull request.
  get <id>                                                                  Get details about a pull request.
  search                                                                    Search for pull requests.
  update <id>                                                               Update a pull request.
  link <workitem-id>                                                        Link a work item to a pull request.
  property                                                                  Operates on pull request properties.
  reviewer                                                                  Reviewer changes and votes.
  vote <Approved|ApprovedWithSuggestions|NoVote|Rejected|WaitingForAuthor>  Vote (approve or reject) a pull request.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Pull request commands

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  comment <comment>                                                         Post a comment on a pull request.
  create                                                                    Creates a new pull request.
  get <id>                                                                  Get details about a pull request.
  search                                                                    Search for pull requests.
  update <id>                                                               Update a pull request.
  link <workitem-id>                                                        Link a work item to a pull request.
  property                                                                  Operates on pull request properties.
  reviewer                                                                  Reviewer changes and votes.
  vote <Approved|ApprovedWithSuggestions|NoVote|Rejected|WaitingForAuthor>  Vote (approve or reject) a pull request.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Post a comment on a pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr comment [<comment>] [options]

Arguments:
  <comment>  The comment to post. Markdown format. If not specified, STDIN will be used.

Options:
  --state <Active|Closed|Pending|Resolved|Unknown|WontFix>  The state to set the new comment.
  --type <CodeChange|System|Text|Unknown>                   The type of comment to post. [default: Text]
  --what-if                                                 Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                                                 Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>                             The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)                            The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)                            The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)                                  The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  --pull-request <pull-request> (REQUIRED)                  The ID of the pull request.
  -?, -h, --help                                            Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Creates a new pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr create [options]

Options:
  --title <title> (REQUIRED)      The title of the pull request.
  --description <description>     The description of the pull request. If an argument for this option is not specified on the command line, it will be pulled in from STDIN.
  --source <source> (REQUIRED)    The name of the branch to merge from. This should not include the refs/heads/ prefix.
  --target <target> (REQUIRED)    The name of the branch to merge into. This should not include the refs/heads/ prefix.
  --draft                         Whether the pull request is a draft.
  --labels <labels>               Labels to apply to the pull request.
  --what-if                       Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                       Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>   The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)        The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Creates a new pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr create [options]

Options:
  --title <title> (REQUIRED)      The title of the pull request.
  --description <description>     The description of the pull request. If an argument for this option is not specified on the command line, it will be pulled in from STDIN.
  --source <source> (REQUIRED)    The name of the branch to merge from. This should not include the refs/heads/ prefix.
  --target <target> (REQUIRED)    The name of the branch to merge into. This should not include the refs/heads/ prefix.
  --draft                         Whether the pull request is a draft.
  --labels <labels>               Labels to apply to the pull request.
  --what-if                       Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                       Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>   The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)  The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)        The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Operates on pull request properties.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr property [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <path> <value>  Set a property to a pull request.
  remove <path>       Remove a property from a pull request.

commands.payload:
Description:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Set a property to a pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr property add <path> [<value>] [options]

Arguments:
  <path>   The path to the property to set. This should always start with a '/' character.
  <value>  The value of the property to set. Should be a JSON token. If not specified, STDIN will be used.

Options:
  --what-if                                 Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                                 Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>             The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)                  The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  --pull-request <pull-request> (REQUIRED)  The ID of the pull request.
  -?, -h, --help                            Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Set a property to a pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr property add <path> [<value>] [options]

Arguments:
  <path>   The path to the property to set. This should always start with a '/' character.
  <value>  The value of the property to set. Should be a JSON token. If not specified, STDIN will be used.

Options:
  --what-if                                 Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                                 Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>             The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)                  The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  --pull-request <pull-request> (REQUIRED)  The ID of the pull request.
  -?, -h, --help                            Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Adds reviewers to a pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr reviewer add [<optional-reviewers>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <optional-reviewers>  The users to add as reviewers to the pull request. These should take the form of user@domain.com.

Options:
  --required <required>                     The users to add as required reviewers to the pull request. These should take the form of user@domain.com. All users listed after this switch will be required.
  --what-if                                 Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                                 Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>             The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)                  The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  --pull-request <pull-request> (REQUIRED)  The ID of the pull request.
  -?, -h, --help                            Show help and usage information

Description:
  Search for pull requests.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr search [options]

Options:
  --source-branch <source-branch>        The source branch of the pull request. MAY start with 'refs/heads/'.
  --target-branch <target-branch>        The target branch of the pull request. MAY start with 'refs/heads/'.
  --status <Abandoned|Active|Completed>  The status of the pull request.
  --top <top>                            The maximum number of pull requests to return.
  --skip <skip>                          The number of pull requests to skip.
  --what-if                              Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                              Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>          The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)         The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)         The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)               The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  -?, -h, --help                         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Update a pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr update [<id>] [options]

Arguments:
  <id>  The ID of the pull request.

Options:
  --title <title>                                             The title of the pull request.
  --description <description>                                 The description of the pull request. If an argument for this option is not specified on the command line, it will be pulled in from STDIN.
  --target-branch <target-branch>                             The target branch of the pull request. This MAY include the refs/heads/ prefix.
  --auto-complete                                             Configures the pull request to be automatically completed when all policies are satisfied.
  --merge-strategy <NoFastForward|Rebase|RebaseMerge|Squash>  Specifies the merge strategy to use for auto-complete.
  --delete-source-branch                                      Whether to delete the source branch after the pull request is completed.
  --status <Abandoned|Active|Completed>                       Activates or abandons the pull request.
  --what-if                                                   Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                                                   Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>                               The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)                              The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)                              The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)                                    The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  -?, -h, --help                                              Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Vote (approve or reject) a pull request.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools azdo pr vote <vote> [options]

Arguments:
  <Approved|ApprovedWithSuggestions|NoVote|Rejected|WaitingForAuthor>  The vote to cast on the specified pull request.

Options:
  --id <id>                                 The ID of the account to cast a vote on behalf of. May be the account ID (guid) or user@domain.com.
  --what-if                                 Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose                                 Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  --access-token <access-token>             The access token to use to authenticate against the AzDO REST API. If not specified but the SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN environment variable is set, that value will be used. Otherwise the tool will attempt to acquire a token automatically from Visual Studio or Windows credentials.
  --account <account> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO account (organization) or URI (e.g. "fabrikamfiber" or "https://dev.azure.com/fabrikamfiber/". Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_COLLECTIONURI environment variable will be used if set.
  --project <project> (REQUIRED)            The AzDO project. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the SYSTEM_TEAMPROJECT environment variable will be used if set.
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)                  The name of the repo. Can also be inferred from the git origin remote URL. If not specified, the value of the BUILD_REPOSITORY_NAME environment variable will be used if set.
  --pull-request <pull-request> (REQUIRED)  The ID of the pull request.
  -?, -h, --help                            Show help and usage information

  Git repo maintenance workflows

  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools git [command] [options]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Removes local branches that have already been merged into some target ref. Squashed branches can sometimes also be detected.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools git trim <mergeTarget> [options]

Arguments:
  <mergeTarget>  Branches become trimmable after they have been merged into this ref. Typically origin/main or similar.

Options:
  --what-if       Prints what would be done without actually doing it.
  --verbose       Prints the command lines of sub-processes spawned by the tool and HTTP requests made.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  NuGet maintenance commands

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  reconcile-versions              Resolves all package downgrade warnings.
  upgrade <id> <version>          Upgrade a package dependency, and all transitive dependencies such that no package downgrade warnings occur.
  trim                            Removes PackageReference items that are redundant because they are to packages that already appear as transitive dependencies.
  ManagePackageVersionsCentrally  Migrates a repo to use centralized package versions.

commands.payload:
Description:
  NuGet maintenance commands

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  reconcile-versions              Resolves all package downgrade warnings.
  upgrade <id> <version>          Upgrade a package dependency, and all transitive dependencies such that no package downgrade warnings occur.
  trim                            Removes PackageReference items that are redundant because they are to packages that already appear as transitive dependencies.
  ManagePackageVersionsCentrally  Migrates a repo to use centralized package versions.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Migrates a repo to use centralized package versions.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget ManagePackageVersionsCentrally [options]

Options:
  --path <path>            The path to the project or repo to upgrade. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-nerdbank.dotnetrepotools-1.2.1-e6e23ac361de494689c2774c01d73080]
  --repo-root <repo-root>  The path to the directory where the Directory.Packages.props is to be created. Defaults to the git repo root above the location specified by --path.
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Resolves all package downgrade warnings.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget reconcile-versions [options]

Options:
  --path <path>                                                  The path to the project or repo to resolve version issues with.
  -f, --framework <framework>                                    The target framework used to evaluate package dependencies. [default: netstandard2.0]
  --disregard-version-properties <disregard-version-properties>  Specifies one or more MSBuild properties that may be used to define a PackageVersion item's Version attribute that should no longer be referenced. This may be useful when properties have been used for multiple packages and their continued use is problematic because the packages now need their own distinct versions.
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Resolves all package downgrade warnings.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget reconcile-versions [options]

Options:
  --path <path>                                                  The path to the project or repo to resolve version issues with.
  -f, --framework <framework>                                    The target framework used to evaluate package dependencies. [default: netstandard2.0]
  --disregard-version-properties <disregard-version-properties>  Specifies one or more MSBuild properties that may be used to define a PackageVersion item's Version attribute that should no longer be referenced. This may be useful when properties have been used for multiple packages and their continued use is problematic because the packages now need their own distinct versions.
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Removes PackageReference items that are redundant because they are to packages that already appear as transitive dependencies.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget trim [options]

Options:
  --path <path>   The path to the project file with the PackageReference items to trim.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Removes PackageReference items that are redundant because they are to packages that already appear as transitive dependencies.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget trim [options]

Options:
  --path <path>   The path to the project file with the PackageReference items to trim.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upgrade a package dependency, and all transitive dependencies such that no package downgrade warnings occur.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget upgrade <id> <version> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>       The ID of the root package to be upgraded.
  <version>  The version to upgrade to.

Options:
  --path <path> (REQUIRED)                                       The path to the project or repo to upgrade.
  -f, --framework <framework>                                    The target framework used to evaluate package dependencies. [default: netstandard2.0]
  --explode                                                      Add PackageVersion items for every transitive dependency, so that they can be added as direct project dependencies as versions are pre-specified.
  --disregard-version-properties <disregard-version-properties>  Specifies one or more MSBuild properties that may be used to define a PackageVersion item's Version attribute that should no longer be referenced. This may be useful when properties have been used for multiple packages and their continued use is problematic because the packages now need their own distinct versions.
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Upgrade a package dependency, and all transitive dependencies such that no package downgrade warnings occur.

Usage:
  Nerdbank.DotNetRepoTools nuget upgrade <id> <version> [options]

Arguments:
  <id>       The ID of the root package to be upgraded.
  <version>  The version to upgrade to.

Options:
  --path <path> (REQUIRED)                                       The path to the project or repo to upgrade.
  -f, --framework <framework>                                    The target framework used to evaluate package dependencies. [default: netstandard2.0]
  --explode                                                      Add PackageVersion items for every transitive dependency, so that they can be added as direct project dependencies as versions are pre-specified.
  --disregard-version-properties <disregard-version-properties>  Specifies one or more MSBuild properties that may be used to define a PackageVersion item's Version attribute that should no longer be referenced. This may be useful when properties have been used for multiple packages and their continued use is problematic because the packages now need their own distinct versions.
  -?, -h, --help                                                 Show help and usage information
